questions.question: According to the GAD-7, what score suggests anxiety that may benefit from professional evaluation?

options.text: Any score above 0
A score of 10 or higher
Only a perfect score of 21
The GAD-7 cannot indicate when to seek help

questions.explanation: A GAD-7 score of 10 or higher (moderate to severe range) suggests anxiety that may benefit from professional evaluation. Scores of 0-4 indicate minimal anxiety, 5-9 indicate mild anxiety, 10-14 indicate moderate anxiety, and 15-21 indicate severe anxiety.

questions.question: Which of the following is a 'red flag' indicating professional help may be needed?

options.text: Occasional worry about upcoming events
Feeling nervous before a job interview
Persistent worry for 6+ months that interferes with work, relationships, or daily activities
Feeling relief after finishing a stressful project

questions.explanation: Red flags for seeking professional help include persistent worry lasting 6 months or more, anxiety interfering with work, relationships, or daily activities, symptoms not improving despite self-help efforts, or anxiety that feels overwhelming or unmanageable.

questions.question: What is the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline?

options.text: A paid therapy service
A free, confidential, 24/7 crisis support line you can call, text, or chat with
A medication hotline
A service only for people with suicidal thoughts

questions.explanation: The 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline is a free, confidential service available 24/7. You can call or text 988, or chat at 988lifeline.org. It provides support for suicidal thoughts, mental health crises, and substance use crises—for yourself or someone you're worried about.

questions.question: What should you expect regarding anxiety fluctuations after completing this course?

options.text: Anxiety should be completely eliminated forever
Anxiety will fluctuate—this is normal, and bad days don't erase progress
Anxiety will steadily increase
You should never feel any anxiety again

questions.explanation: Progress isn't linear—anxiety will naturally fluctuate, and stressful life events may temporarily increase it. Bad days don't erase your progress. The key signs of lasting progress are noticing anxiety earlier, recovering faster, doing things you previously avoided, and having confidence in your ability to cope.

questions.question: What is the best approach to prevent relapse after making progress with anxiety?

options.text: Stop all practices once you feel better
Keep practicing skills even when feeling good, maintain healthy habits, and seek help early if symptoms return significantly
Avoid all potentially stressful situations forever
Only practice when anxiety is severe

questions.explanation: To prevent relapse, continue practicing skills even when feeling good, don't abandon coping strategies when anxiety is low, maintain healthy lifestyle habits, address new stressors before they escalate, and seek help early if symptoms return significantly. Consistency is key to maintaining progress.

title: Long-Term Management and When to Seek Help - Quiz
